section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has vascular disease in their legs.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Vascular disease is documented as an established condition rather than a symptom description; it functions more as an active problem/diagnostic impression than as patient-reported current symptoms.
Peripheral neuropathy is a diagnostic label/problem list item; while phrased as a report, it represents a known diagnosis better suited to the Assessment section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor asks if the patient is still smoking.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a clinician query/observation about risk behavior rather than a patient-reported symptom or history detail; it fits better as part of documented findings or social history than HPI.
